vocabulary.definition: Graphics Interchange Format — an animated or static image format widely used for reaction and expressive content on the web.
A transparent-background image or animation used for expressive overlays in messaging and social applications.
A Tenor API object representing a single GIF or sticker item, including its media formats, metadata, and URLs.
A map of format names (e.g., gif, mp4, tinygif) to their corresponding media objects, each containing URL, dimensions, and file size.
A safety filter parameter controlling the maturity level of returned content. Values: off, low, medium, high.
A filter to restrict returned content type. Accepted values include sticker, static, and -static.
Aspect ratio filter. Values: all (no constraint), wide (0.42–2.36), standard (0.56–1.78).
A pagination cursor returned by the API in the `next` field, used to retrieve subsequent pages of results.
A developer-defined string identifying the integration using the API key, enabling differentiation across multiple apps.
A BCP 47-style locale string (e.g., en_US) used to interpret search terms in the appropriate language.
A list of search terms currently trending on Tenor, updated hourly by Tenor's AI.
An API call that records when a user shares a specific GIF, used to improve Tenor's recommendation AI.
